title: Joy
justification: This talk offers the most focused and comprehensive treatment of joy as a practice, covering the enemies of joy (fear of indulgence and indulgence itself), the distinction between joy and excitement, practical exercises for developing joy, the connection to mudita (appreciative joy), and concrete meditation instructions. It includes memorable anecdotes (the ascetic monk with buffalo curd, Sayadaw Ujanaka at Beatenberg) and provides a clear structure moving from obstacles to cultivation to practice, making it ideal as a chapter spine.
summary: Bhante Bodhidhamma explores why joy is rarely taught despite being essential to the Buddhist path, examining how both fear of indulgence and indulgence itself destroy joy. He offers practical exercises for recognizing daily joys, developing appreciative joy for others, and using joy as a restorative practice, culminating in meditation instructions for cultivating joyful states.

relevance.note: Entirely about cultivating joy and muditā
